parts.name: Part 1
parts.instructions: You must answer this question. Write 140-190 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: In your English class you have been talking about school rules. Now your English teacher has asked you to write an essay. Write an essay using all the notes and giving reasons for your point of view.

stimulus.body:
"All schools should require their students to wear a uniform. Do you agree?"

Notes

Write about:
1. cost for families
2. equality between students
3. ............... (your own idea)

bullets: give reasons for your point of view

parts.name: Part 2
parts.instructions: Answer one of these questions. Write 140-190 words in an appropriate style.

tasks.lead: You see this announcement on an English-language website for teenagers.

stimulus.title: Online clothes shops — the truth
stimulus.body: Teenagers buy more and more clothes online, but the photos don't always match what arrives in the post! Write a review of an online clothes shop you have used, describing what you ordered, how well the shop handled delivery and returns, and whether you would order from it again.

bullets: how the shop handled delivery and returns

tasks.lead: You have received this email from your English-speaking friend Jo.

stimulus.body:
From: Jo
Subject: Uniform?!

Disaster — my new school has a uniform, and I've never worn one in my life! Your school has one, doesn't it? What's it actually like, wearing the same thing every day? Is there anything good about it at all? And how do people still manage to look different from each other?
Help!
Jo

bullets: what wearing a uniform is really like
how students still manage to look different

tasks.lead: Your school's student council is discussing the uniform rules and has asked you to write a report for the school management.

stimulus.title: Report: student opinion on the uniform rules
stimulus.body: Describe what students think about the current uniform rules, identify the rule that causes most complaints, and recommend one change, explaining how it would benefit both students and the school.

bullets: what students think about the current rules
the rule that causes most complaints
one recommended change, with its benefits
